items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today

items.title: Capital B shareholders approve up to $120B in financing capacity for Bitcoin strategy
items.summary: Capital B shareholders approved up to $120 billion in financing capacity, including equity and credit instruments, to support the company's Bitcoin accumulation strategy.

items.title: Ireland Tightens Crypto Safeguards in New Financial Crime Action Plan
items.summary: A new National Risk Assessment names crypto-asset misuse among Ireland's top threats, as a 30-point plan tightens checks on crypto funds.

items.title: G7 calls for joint action on North Korean crypto theft, cybercrime
items.summary: G7 leaders broadened their warning over North Korean crypto theft to include wider cybercrime as researchers link DPRK-affiliated actors to billions of dollars in stolen digital assets.

items.title: CME to Sue CFTC Over Bitcoin Perpetual Futures Approval: CEO
items.summary: Outgoing CME chief Terry Duffy says perpetual futures are actually swaps under Dodd-Frank, and that the exchange will file suit Thursday.

items.summary: The MFSA is seeking feedback on whether decentralization should be assessed as a spectrum rather than a binary concept.

items.title: Bitcoin market cap rebound to take '5-10 years' after dropping 10 places since mid-2025

items.summary: The bond market is sending a signal that complicates prospects of a near-term bitcoin bull run.

items.title: Ryan Salame’s wife to face charges over FTX-funded congressional run
items.summary: A federal judge has denied a bid from Michelle Bond, the wife of former FTX executive Ryan Salame, to have campaign finance charges against her dismissed.

items.title: Florida man pleads guilty for promoting $1.8B ‘HyperFund’ crypto fraud
items.summary: Rodney “Bitcoin Rodney” Burton faces a maximum sentence of five years in federal prison for conspiracy to operate an unlicensed money transmitting business.

items.title: Live markets: DXY Index breaks higher as bitcoin tries to weather stronger dollar
items.summary: Total crypto market value has held steady near $2.26 trillion since Tuesday, with the recovery losing momentum after the Fed killed rate-cut hopes and spot ETFs swung back to outflows.

items.title: Strategy's STRC preferred stock hits a record low below par
items.summary: The slide has paused the above-par share sales Strategy uses to fund bitcoin purchases, and it is the same stock whose dividends forced the company's first BTC sale this month.

items.title: Dorsey’s Block says new AI tool handles 15% of code work
items.summary: Builderbot is the “missing layer between AI coding tools and how engineering actually works at scale,” said Brad Axen, head of AI capabilities at Block.

items.title: XRP slips 4% below $1.20 after breakout rally stalls near key resistance
items.summary: Heavy selling pushed XRP back through a closely watched support level, though buyers stepped in above $1.17 to prevent a deeper pullback.

items.title: Buying bitcoin below its 200-week average has historically delivered over 100% in median returns, Kraken says
items.summary: Bitcoin briefly slipped below its 200-week moving average twice in the past two weeks, a rare event that Kraken says has historically marked strong entry points for buyers.

items.title: Bitcoin, ether slide after a hawkish Fed, even as Trump's signed Iran deal lifts stocks
items.summary: The Fed held rates but signaled it is more worried about inflation than growth in Chair Kevin Warsh's first meeting.

items.title: France to stop certifying products lacking quantum-resistant encryption
items.summary: France’s cybersecurity agency plans to block certification of products without quantum-resistant encryption starting in 2027, with full adoption targeted for 2030.

items.title: Next bull run will be slower, less volatile as investors' crypto appetite evolves, Bitwise CIO says
items.summary: Long-time bitcoin bull Matt Hougan told CoinDesk that during this bear market and with ‘doubts swirling’, investors have found ‘it easier to reach for something more tangible:’ stablecoins and tokenization.

items.title: CME chief executive says company plans to sue CFTC after perpetual futures approval
items.summary: Kalshi's perpetual futures product did not meet the Dodd-Frank Act's definition of a "swap" and should not have been approved, Terrence Duffy said.

items.title: Kentucky sues Kalshi, Polymarket, joining prediction market legal battle
items.summary: Kentucky has sued Polymarket, Kalshi and Kalshi’s partners Coinbase, Robinhood and Webull, over offering sports event contracts in the state.

items.title: Tether winds down gold-backed derivative stablecoin aUSDT
items.summary: Tether is focusing on stronger user demand, deeper liquidity, and broader long-term market opportunity for its leading products.

items.title: France to Phase Out Non-Quantum Encryption as Bitcoin Security Concerns Grow
items.summary: French authorities said that government cybersecurity researchers will stop certifying security products that lack quantum-resistant encryption beginning in 2027.

items.title: Crypto market treads thin ice following Warsh FOMC, Trump Iran comments
items.summary: Markets wobbled after mixed comments from President Trump on the Iran peace deal and Fed Chair Kevin Warsh signaling a new direction from the Federal Reserve.

items.title: Psychologists Say Patients Are Bringing AI Into Therapy Sessions: Survey
items.summary: A new APA survey found that more than a third of psychologists have patients using AI as an additional mental health professional, even as clinicians warn that the technology can reinforce delusions.

items.title: Michigan Federal Judge Rules Sports Prediction Markets Are Not Under CFTC Purview
items.summary: The judge said Polymarket is not likely to succeed on the merits of its case against Michigan state regulators.

items.title: Gaming groups urge Congress to ban prediction markets sports betting in CLARITY Act
items.summary: Gambling industry groups want the US Senate to step in to clarify that the Commodity Futures Trading Commission does not have the authority to oversee prediction markets.

items.title: SpaceX Surge Could Be Worth Billions for FTX—Will Creditors Benefit?
items.summary: Defunct crypto exchange FTX gained exposure to SpaceX in 2022, and the rocket-maker’s post-IPO rally has lifted hopes among creditors.

items.title: Kentucky targets prediction markets, puts Trump-aligned state in potential clash with president's team
items.summary: President Donald Trump has taken the stance that states have no business with firms like Kalshi and Polymarket, and now a staunch GOP state is defying that view.

items.title: Crypto-backed GOP candidate wins Alabama Senate runoff with June primaries looming
items.summary: Several more US states are scheduled to hold primaries next week after one Republican candidate won in Alabama on Tuesday after a crypto company-backed PAC used what it called its “biggest spend of the cycle.”

items.title: Fidelity joins Wall Street's race to manage stablecoin reserves
items.summary: Following State Street, Fidelity is targeting reserve assets that underpin the expanding stablecoin market.

items.title: Bitcoin capitulation ‘twice as weak’ after spot liquidity turns supportive: Glassnode
items.summary: Bitcoin’s realized losses fell by 46% as increasing bid-side liquidity points to easing sell pressure. Can bulls push BTC price back above $70,000?

items.title: Nvidia Built Robots That Train Themselves Using AI Coding Agents
items.summary: Nvidia's ENPIRE hands an entire robot fleet to coding agents like Codex and Claude Code, letting them write training code, test it on real hardware, and improve without a human watching.

items.title: Bybit lands on Singapore MAS Investor Alert List
items.summary: Crypto exchange Bybit has been added to Singapore’s MAS Investor Alert List, which flags entities that may be mistakenly perceived as licensed or regulated.
